records.ety: [OF. asai, essai, trial, F. essa. See Essay, n.]
usage: Assay and essay are radically the same word; but modern usage has appropriated assay chiefly to experiments in metallurgy, and essay to intellectual and bodily efforts. See Essay.

quotes: I am withal persuaded that it may prove much more easy in the assay than it now seems at distance.

defs: Examination and determination; test; as, an assay of bread or wine.
quotes: This can not be, by no assay of reason.

defs: Trial by danger or by affliction; adventure; risk; hardship; state of being tried.
quotes: Through many hard assays which did betide.
With gold and pearl of rich assay.

defs: The act or process of ascertaining the proportion of a particular metal in an ore or alloy; especially, the determination of the proportion of gold or silver in bullion or coin.
The alloy or metal to be assayed.
notes: ☞ Assay is used adjectively or as the first part of a compound; as, assay balance, assay furnace.

records.ety: [OF. asaier, essaier, F. essayer, fr. essai. See Assay, n., Essay, v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Assayed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Assaying.]

defs: To try; to attempt; to apply.
quotes: To-night let us assay our plot.
Soft words to his fierce passion she assayed.
When the heart is ill assayed.

defs: To try tasting, as food or drink.

To subject, as an ore, alloy, or other metallic compound, to chemical or metallurgical examination, in order to determine the amount of a particular metal contained in it, or to ascertain its composition.
